description_long: This is a freestanding wooden awning structure with a pitched, gabled roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The roof is supported by four sturdy vertical wooden posts, each anchored at the base with a concrete or stone footing. The frame features decorative wooden trusses and braces, adding both structural integrity and visual interest. The underside of the roof reveals exposed beams and cross-bracing, typical of timber-framed outdoor shelters. The overall design is simple yet robust, suitable for providing shade and shelter in parks, gardens, or public spaces.
description: A freestanding wooden awning with a pitched, shingled roof and sturdy post-and-beam construction, designed for outdoor shelter.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open entryway, decorative trusses, and the full gabled roof supported by four posts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the shingled roof and the depth of the structure, with visible post and beam connections.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back truss and the symmetry of the roof, with the posts and braces clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the roof's length and the structural support beneath, mirroring the other side.
attribute_support_structure_type: fixed frame with posts and beams
